div.h1: Pointer Aliasing: What the Compiler Must Prove

text.tspan: // The compiler is NOT stupid—it's CONSERVATIVE.
// It must preserve EXACT program semantics.
// Without restrict: "I cannot PROVE these don't alias,
// so I must assume they MIGHT."
// With restrict: "The programmer PROMISES no alias.
// WARNING: If you lie with restrict and pointers DO alias,
// the compiler generates wrong code silently.
// The compiler is NOT stupid—it's CONSERVATIVE.
// It must preserve EXACT program semantics.
// Without restrict: "I cannot PROVE these don't alias,
// so I must assume they MIGHT."
// With restrict: "The programmer PROMISES no alias.
// WARNING: If you lie with restrict and pointers DO alias,
// the compiler generates wrong code silently.
